code: # Basic usage - with trip status and duration calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   catch_total = c(5, 3, 7, 2),   hours_fished = c(2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 1.5),   trip_status = c("complete", "complete", "incomplete", "complete"),   trip_duration = c(2.0, 2.5, 1.5, 1.5) )  design_with_interviews <- add_interviews(   design, interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 interviews: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 interviews #> • Stratum weekend: 2 interviews #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> ℹ Added 4 interviews: 3 complete (75%), 1 incomplete (25%) print(design_with_interviews) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 4 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-04) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: "none" #> Interviews: 4 observations #> Type: "access" #> Catch: catch_total #> Effort: hours_fished #> Trip status: 3 complete, 1 incomplete #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Sections: "none"  # With harvest column and calculated duration from timestamps interviews2 <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   catch_total = c(5, 3, 7, 2),   catch_kept = c(2, 1, 5, 2),   hours_fished = c(2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 1.5),   trip_status = c("complete", "incomplete", "complete", "complete"),   trip_start = as.POSIXct(c(     "2024-06-01 08:00", "2024-06-02 09:00",     "2024-06-03 07:00", "2024-06-04 10:00"   )),   interview_time = as.POSIXct(c(     "2024-06-01 10:00", "2024-06-02 11:30",     "2024-06-03 10:00", "2024-06-04 11:30"   )) )  design2 <- add_interviews(   design, interviews2,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   harvest = catch_kept,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_start = trip_start,   interview_time = interview_time ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 interviews: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 interviews #> • Stratum weekend: 2 interviews #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> ℹ Added 4 interviews: 3 complete (75%), 1 incomplete (25%)

what: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: Constructs creel_design object calendar data tidy column selection. entry point creel survey analysis workflows. design object stores survey structure (date, strata, optional site), validates input data (Tier 1 validation), serves foundation adding count data estimating effort. bus-route surveys nonuniform site selection probabilities, use survey_type = "bus_route" supply sampling_frame data frame specifying sites, circuits, sampling probabilities.

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
code: creel_design(   calendar,   date,   strata,   site = NULL,   design_type = "instantaneous",   survey_type = design_type,   sampling_frame = NULL,   p_site = NULL,   p_period = NULL,   circuit = NULL,   effort_type = NULL,   camera_mode = NULL,   h_open = NULL,   visibility_correction = NULL )

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: calendar data frame containing calendar data date strata columns. Must least one Date column one character/factor column (validated via internal schema check). date Tidy selector date column. Must select exactly one column class Date. Accepts bare column names tidyselect helpers (e.g., starts_with("date")). strata Tidy selector strata columns. Can select one columns class character factor. Accepts bare column names tidyselect helpers (e.g., c(day_type, season) starts_with("day")). site Optional tidy selector site column. instantaneous designs, selects calendar. bus-route designs (survey_type = "bus_route"), selects site ID column sampling_frame. Must select exactly one column class character factor. Default NULL (single-site survey instantaneous designs; required bus-route designs). design_type Character string specifying survey design type. Default "instantaneous". Kept backward compatibility; use survey_type new code. survey_type Character string specifying survey type. Default inherits design_type ("instantaneous"). Use "bus_route" nonuniform probability bus-route surveys (BUSRT-06, BUSRT-07). survey_type design_type refer concept; survey_type canonical parameter new designs. sampling_frame Data frame site, circuit, probability columns. Required survey_type = "bus_route". row represents one site-circuit sampling unit inclusion probability components (p_site p_period). p_site Tidy selector site sampling probability column sampling_frame. Required survey_type = "bus_route". Values must (0, 1] must sum 1.0 within circuit (tolerance 1e-6). p_period Tidy selector period sampling probability column sampling_frame, scalar numeric value (0, 1] applies globally rows. Required survey_type = "bus_route". circuit Optional tidy selector circuit ID column sampling_frame. circuit route x period combination. omitted, rows treated belonging single unnamed circuit (".default"). Required multi-circuit designs. effort_type Character string specifying type effort measured ice fishing surveys. Required survey_type = "ice". Must one "time_on_ice" (total hours angler ice) "active_fishing_time" (hours actively fishing, excluding travel/setup). value controls column name estimate_effort() output: total_effort_hr_on_ice total_effort_hr_active. camera_mode Character string specifying camera sub-mode. Required survey_type = "camera". Must one "counter" (camera records daily ingress total) "ingress_egress" (camera records individual arrival/departure timestamps, preprocessed preprocess_camera_timestamps() calling add_counts()). h_open Positive numeric scalar specifying number hours fishery open per day. Required survey_type = "aerial". Used expansion factor aerial effort estimator: \(\hat{E} = N_{obs} \times h_{open} / v\). visibility_correction Optional numeric scalar (0, 1] specifying proportion anglers water detectable aircraft. Used survey_type = "aerial". Defaults 1.0 (anglers visible) NULL. value 0.85 means 85% anglers detected; effort estimate scaled \(1 / 0.85\).

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: creel_design S3 object (list) components: calendar original calendar data frame date_col Character name date column strata_cols Character vector strata column names site_col Character name site column, NULL design_type Character design type counts NULL (populated add_counts() future) survey NULL (populated internally estimation) bus_route List resolved sampling frame data column mappings, NULL non-bus-route designs. Contains: $data (sampling frame .pi_i column added), $site_col, $circuit_col, $p_site_col, $p_period_col, $pi_i_col (always ".pi_i").

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: constructor performs fail-fast validation: Date column class Date (character, numeric, POSIXct) Date column contains NA values Strata columns character factor (numeric, logical) Site column (provided) character factor (bus_route ) p_site p_period values (0, 1] (bus_route ) p_site values sum 1.0 within circuit (tolerance 1e-6) (bus_route ) p_period values constant within circuit (tolerance 1e-10)

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: Jones, C. M., & Pollock, K. H. (2012). Recreational survey methods: estimating effort, harvest, abundance. . V. Zale, D. L. Parrish, & T. M. Sutton (Eds.), Fisheries Techniques (3rd ed., pp. 883–919). American Fisheries Society. Eq. 19.4 19.5 define bus-route estimators; pp. 883–884 define inclusion probability \(\pi_i = p_{\text{site}} \times p_{\text{period}}\).

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
code: # Basic design with single stratum calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  # Multiple strata calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   season = c("summer", "summer") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = c(day_type, season))  # With site column for multi-site survey calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   lake = c("lake_a", "lake_b") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type, site = lake)  # Using tidyselect helpers calendar <- data.frame(   survey_date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   day_period = c("morning", "evening") ) design <- creel_design(   calendar,   date = starts_with("survey"),   strata = starts_with("day") )  # Bus-route design with scalar p_period calendar_br <- data.frame(   date = as.Date("2024-06-01"),   day_type = "weekday" ) sf <- data.frame(   site = c("A", "B", "C"),   p_site = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),   p_period = 0.5 ) design_br <- creel_design(   calendar_br,   date = date,   strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route",   sampling_frame = sf,   site = site,   p_site = p_site,   p_period = p_period )

what: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: Determines number interviews needed achieve target coefficient variation CPUE (catch-per-unit-effort) ratio estimate, using ratio-estimator variance approximation Cochran (1977).

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
code: creel_n_cpue(cv_catch, cv_effort, rho = 0, cv_target)

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: cv_catch Numeric scalar. Pilot coefficient variation catch per interview (numerator CPUE ratio). Must > 0. cv_effort Numeric scalar. Pilot coefficient variation effort per interview (denominator CPUE ratio). Must > 0. rho Numeric scalar. Pilot correlation catch effort per interview. Must [-1, 1]. Default 0 (conservative; -estimates required n catch effort positively correlated). cv_target Numeric scalar. Target coefficient variation CPUE estimate. Must (0, 1].

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: integer scalar (>= 1): number interviews required.

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: Implements ratio-estimator variance approximation Cochran (1977, Chapter 6) parameterised terms coefficients variation rather raw variances, natural pre-season planning: $$n = \left\lceil \frac{CV_{catch}^2 + CV_{effort}^2   - 2 \rho \cdot CV_{catch} \cdot CV_{effort}}{CV_{target}^2} \right\rceil$$ Setting rho = 0 (default) conservative: -estimates required sample size catch effort positively correlated. Users pilot data supply observed correlation obtain less conservative estimate. result floored 1L ensure least one interview recommended.

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: Cochran, W.G. 1977. Sampling Techniques, 3rd ed. Wiley, New York. Chapter 6 (ratio estimator variance approximation).

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
code: # rho = 0 (conservative, no pilot correlation data) creel_n_cpue(cv_catch = 0.8, cv_effort = 0.5, rho = 0, cv_target = 0.20) #> [1] 23  # With known positive correlation (smaller n) creel_n_cpue(cv_catch = 0.8, cv_effort = 0.5, rho = 0.5, cv_target = 0.20) #> [1] 13

what: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: Uses stratified sample size formula McCormick & Quist (2017) determine many sampling days needed achieve target coefficient variation effort estimate, given pilot variance estimates per day-type stratum.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
code: creel_n_effort(cv_target, N_h, ybar_h, s2_h)

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: cv_target Numeric scalar. Target coefficient variation effort estimate (e.g., 0.20 20 percent). Must (0, 1]. N_h Named numeric vector. Total available days per stratum (e.g., c(weekday = 65, weekend = 28)). Values must >= 1. ybar_h Numeric vector length N_h. Pilot mean effort per day per stratum (e.g., angler-hours per day). Values must >= 0. s2_h Numeric vector length N_h. Pilot variance effort per day per stratum. Values must >= 0.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: named integer vector. Elements named strata N_h give sampling days required per stratum; element "total" gives overall sample size proportional allocation.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: Implements Cochran (1977) equation 5.25 proportional allocation, applied creel surveys McCormick & Quist (2017). finite-population correction (FPC) factor intentionally omitted (standard practice pre-season planning goal determine many days sample, assess precision completed survey). per-stratum sample sizes n_h computed total n_total proportional allocation: n_h = ceiling(n_total * N_h / sum(N_h)). stratum ceiling-ed independently, sum(n_h) may exceed n_total.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: McCormick, J.L. Quist, M.C. 2017. Sample size estimation -site creel surveys. North American Journal Fisheries Management 37:970-983. doi:10.1080/02755947.2017.1342723 Cochran, W.G. 1977. Sampling Techniques, 3rd ed. Wiley, New York.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
code: # Two-stratum weekday/weekend example creel_n_effort(   cv_target = 0.20,   N_h = c(weekday = 65, weekend = 28),   ybar_h = c(50, 60),   s2_h = c(400, 500) ) #> weekday weekend   total  #>       3       2       4

what: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: Calculates probability detecting fractional change CPUE given target sample size per season, historical CV, significance level. Uses two-sample normal approximation equal group sizes.

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
code: creel_power(   n,   cv_historical,   delta_pct,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "one.sided") )

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: n Integerish scalar (>= 1). Number interviews per season. cv_historical Numeric scalar (> 0). Coefficient variation CPUE historical pilot data. delta_pct Numeric scalar (> 0). Fractional change detect, expressed proportion — e.g., 0.20 20 percent change. Note: fraction, percentage point. alpha Numeric scalar (0, 0.5]. Type error rate. Default 0.05. alternative Character. Either "two.sided" (default) "one.sided".

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: numeric scalar (0, 1): estimated statistical power.

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: Implements two-sample normal approximation power equal group sizes, parameterised terms CV: $$ncp = |\delta| \cdot \sqrt{n/2} \, / \, CV_{historical}$$ alternative = "two.sided": $$power = \Phi(ncp - z_{\alpha/2}) + \Phi(-ncp - z_{\alpha/2})$$ alternative = "one.sided": $$power = \Phi(ncp - z_{\alpha})$$ delta fractional effect size (delta_pct), n number interviews per season, CV_historical pilot CV CPUE. warning issued delta_pct > 5 values greater 5 almost certainly input percentage-point form rather fractional form (e.g., 20 instead 0.20).

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: Cohen, J. 1988. Statistical Power Analysis Behavioral Sciences, 2nd ed. Lawrence Erlbaum Associates, Hillsdale, NJ.

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
code: # Two-sided power at n = 100, CV = 0.5, 20 percent change creel_power(n = 100, cv_historical = 0.5, delta_pct = 0.20) #> [1] 0.8074304  # One-sided test (higher power for same inputs) creel_power(n = 100, cv_historical = 0.5, delta_pct = 0.20, alternative = "one.sided") #> [1] 0.881709

what: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: Calculates coefficient variation attainable given fixed sample size, acting algebraic inverse creel_n_effort() (type = "effort") creel_n_cpue() (type = "cpue").

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
code: cv_from_n(type = c("effort", "cpue"), n, ...)

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: type Character. Either "effort" "cpue". Selects formula branch required additional arguments. n Integerish scalar (>= 1). Available sample size (sampling days "effort", interviews "cpue"). ... Additional arguments passed relevant branch: type = "effort": N_h — Named numeric vector; total available days per stratum (>= 1). ybar_h — Numeric vector; pilot mean effort per day per stratum (>= 0). s2_h — Numeric vector; pilot variance effort per day per stratum (>= 0). type = "cpue": cv_catch — Numeric scalar; pilot CV catch per interview (> 0). cv_effort — Numeric scalar; pilot CV effort per interview (> 0). rho — Numeric scalar; pilot correlation catch effort, [-1, 1]. Default 0.

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: numeric scalar (> 0): expected CV achievable sample size n.

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: Effort branch (type = "effort"): $$CV = \frac{\sqrt{\sum_h N_h s_h^2 / n}}{\sum_h N_h \bar{y}_h}$$ inverse Cochran (1977) stratified sample-size formula implemented creel_n_effort(). CPUE branch (type = "cpue"): $$CV = \sqrt{(CV_{catch}^2 + CV_{effort}^2   - 2\rho \cdot CV_{catch} \cdot CV_{effort}) / n}$$ inverse ratio-estimator formula implemented creel_n_cpue(). creel_n_effort() creel_n_cpue() apply ceiling(), round-trip property cv_from_n(type, n = creel_n_*(cv, ...), ...) <= cv (recovered CV target).

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: Cochran, W.G. 1977. Sampling Techniques, 3rd ed. Wiley, New York.

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n

text: design creel_design object counts (via add_counts) interviews (via add_interviews) attached. count interview survey objects must exist. Optional tidy selector grouping variables. specified, must match across effort CPUE estimates (calendar strata interview variables). Accepts bare column names, multiple columns, tidyselect helpers. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method: "taylor" (default), "bootstrap", "jackknife". Applied effort CPUE estimation, combined via delta method. conf_level Numeric confidence level (default: 0.95) aggregate_sections Logical. design created add_sections, .lake_total row appended sums per-section estimates? Default TRUE. Set FALSE return per-section rows without lake total. missing_sections Character(1). Action registered section absent either count data interview data: "warn" (default) inserts NA row data_available = FALSE, "error" raises hard error. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informational message identifying estimator path used. Default FALSE.

title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
text: creel_estimates S3 object method = "product-total-catch". bus-route designs, returns bus-route HT estimate method = "total" "site_contributions" attribute. sectioned designs, returns per-section rows plus (default) .lake_total row. lake-wide total computed sum(TC_i) sections, never E_total * CPUE_pooled.

title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
text: Total catch computed Effort × CPUE. Variance propagated using delta method, accounts uncertainty estimates. formula independent estimates approximately: $$Var(E \times C) \approx E^2 \cdot Var(C) + C^2 \cdot Var(E)$$ function uses survey::svycontrast() compute variance automatically via symbolic differentiation Taylor series approximation. Sectioned designs: add_sections called design, section estimated independently using count survey (via rebuild_counts_survey) interview survey (via rebuild_interview_survey). lake-wide total arithmetic sum sum(TC_i), E_total * CPUE_pooled. lake-wide SE uses zero-covariance assumption: sqrt(sum(se_i^2)). Cross-section covariance count-based effort interview-based CPUE designs identified therefore assumed zero. Design compatibility requirements: Count data must attached via add_counts() effort estimation Interview data must attached via add_interviews() CPUE estimation Grouped estimation requires identical grouping variables estimates Calendar stratification must shared counts interviews

what: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
text: Converts creel schedule calendar circuit definitions sampling frame tibble inclusion_prob p_period columns ready creel_design(survey_type = "bus_route"). Inclusion probability formula: inclusion_prob = p_site * p_period p_period = crew / n_circuits. n_circuits number distinct circuit values sampling_frame (1 circuit NULL). crew number field crews deployed simultaneously.

title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
code: generate_bus_schedule(   schedule,   sampling_frame,   site,   p_site,   circuit = NULL,   crew,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
text: schedule creel_schedule tibble generate_schedule(). Currently unused computation required ensure caller built valid schedule constructing sampling frame. sampling_frame data frame site p_site columns (optionally circuit). site Column sampling_frame giving site identifiers (tidy selector: bare name, quoted string, tidyselect helper). p_site Column sampling_frame giving per-site selection probability within circuit. Values must sum 1.0 per circuit (tolerance 1e-6). circuit Optional column giving circuit assignment. NULL, sites treated single circuit. crew Integer scalar: number crews field simultaneously. seed Optional integer seed (reserved future randomised designs; currently unused function deterministic).

title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
text: tibble: sampling_frame columns plus p_period inclusion_prob. inclusion_prob = p_site * p_period.

what: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
text: Generates stratified random sampling calendar creel survey season. season divided weekday weekend strata, days randomly selected within stratum. Output creel_schedule tibble ready pass creel_design().

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
code: generate_schedule(   start_date,   end_date,   n_periods,   n_days = NULL,   sampling_rate = NULL,   period_labels = NULL,   expand_periods = TRUE,   include_all = FALSE,   ordered_periods = FALSE,   period_intensity = NULL,   seed )

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
text: start_date Character Date. First day survey season (ISO 8601 "YYYY-MM-DD"). end_date Character Date. Last day survey season (ISO 8601 "YYYY-MM-DD"). n_periods Integer. Number sampling periods per day. n_days Named integer vector days sample per stratum (e.g., c(weekday = 20, weekend = 10)), scalar applied uniformly strata. Mutually exclusive sampling_rate. sampling_rate Named numeric vector sampling fractions per stratum (e.g., c(weekday = 0.3, weekend = 0.6)), scalar applied uniformly strata. Mutually exclusive n_days. period_labels Optional character vector length n_periods human-readable period names. supplied, period_id character (ordered factor ordered_periods = TRUE). expand_periods Logical (default TRUE). TRUE, output one row per sampled day x period (nrow = sampled_days * n_periods). FALSE, output one row per sampled day period_id omitted. include_all Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, season dates returned sampled logical column. FALSE, sampled dates returned. ordered_periods Logical (default FALSE). TRUE period_labels supplied, period_id ordered factor preserving label order. period_intensity yet implemented. Must NULL. seed Integer seed reproducible random day selection. Uses withr::with_seed() avoid mutating global RNG state.

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
text: creel_schedule data frame columns: date (Date): Sampled () dates. day_type (character): "weekday" "weekend". period_id (integer, character, ordered factor): Period within day. Absent expand_periods = FALSE. sampled (logical): Present include_all = TRUE.

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
code: # Basic schedule with stratified sampling rates sched <- generate_schedule(   start_date = "2024-06-01",   end_date = "2024-08-31",   n_periods = 2,   sampling_rate = c(weekday = 0.3, weekend = 0.6),   seed = 42 )  # Use result with creel_design() creel_design(sched, date = date, strata = day_type)

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: Returns enumeration count data (observed interviewed angler counts, expansion factor) interview record bus-route creel_design interviews attached via add_interviews. expansion factor \(n\_counted / n\_interviewed\) accounts anglers present site interviewed. used bus-route effort harvest estimation (Jones & Pollock (2012) Eq. 19.4 19.5).

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
code: get_enumeration_counts(design)

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: design creel_design object design_type = "bus_route" interview data attached via add_interviews.

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: data frame site identifier column, circuit identifier column, n_counted (resolved column name), n_interviewed (resolved column name), .expansion (n_counted / n_interviewed, NA n_interviewed = 0).

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: Jones, C. M., & Pollock, K. H. (2012). Recreational survey methods: estimating effort, harvest, abundance. . V. Zale, D. L. Parrish, & T. M. Sutton (Eds.), Fisheries Techniques (3rd ed., pp. 883–919). American Fisheries Society. Enumeration expansion factor used Eq. 19.4 19.5 bus-route effort harvest estimation.

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
code: cal <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-03", "2024-06-04", "2024-06-05", "2024-06-06")),   day_type = "weekday" ) sf <- data.frame(   site = c("A", "B"),   circuit = c("am", "am"),   p_site = c(0.6, 0.4),   p_period = rep(0.5, 2) ) design_br <- creel_design(   cal,   date = date, strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route", sampling_frame = sf,   site = site, circuit = circuit,   p_site = p_site, p_period = p_period ) interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   site = c("A", "B"), circuit = c("am", "am"),   catch_total = c(3L, 2L), hours_fished = c(2.0, 1.5),   trip_status = c("complete", "complete"),   trip_duration = c(2.0, 1.5),   n_counted = c(5L, 4L), n_interviewed = c(3L, 2L) ) design2 <- add_interviews(   design_br, interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration,   n_counted = n_counted, n_interviewed = n_interviewed ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 1 stratum has fewer than 3 interviews: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 interviews #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> ℹ Added 2 interviews: 2 complete (100%), 0 incomplete (0%) get_enumeration_counts(design2) #>   site circuit n_counted n_interviewed .expansion #> 1    A      am         5             3   1.666667 #> 2    B      am         4             2   2.000000

what: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: Returns computed inclusion probabilities (\(\pi_i = p_{\text{site}} \times p_{\text{period}}\)) site-circuit combination bus-route creel design. inclusion probability represents two-stage sampling probability: probability particular site visited particular sampling period, combining site selection probability within circuit circuit (period) selection probability.

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
code: get_inclusion_probs(design)

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: design creel_design() object created survey_type = "bus_route".

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: data frame three columns: site identifier column, circuit identifier column, .pi_i (computed inclusion probability \(\pi_i = p_{\text{site}} \times p_{\text{period}}\) site-circuit unit). Column names site circuit match resolved column names original sampling frame (.circuit designs without explicit circuit column).

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: Jones, C. M., & Pollock, K. H. (2012). Recreational survey methods: estimating effort, harvest, abundance. . V. Zale, D. L. Parrish, & T. M. Sutton (Eds.), Fisheries Techniques (3rd ed., pp. 883–919). American Fisheries Society. Definition \(\pi_i\) two-stage bus-route sampling, used Eq. 19.4 19.5.

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
code: sf <- data.frame(   site = c("A", "B", "C"),   p_site = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),   p_period = rep(0.5, 3),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) cal <- data.frame(   date = as.Date("2024-06-01"),   day_type = "weekday",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) design <- creel_design(cal,   date = date, strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route", sampling_frame = sf,   site = site, p_site = p_site, p_period = p_period ) get_inclusion_probs(design) #>   site .circuit .pi_i #> 1    A .default  0.15 #> 2    B .default  0.20 #> 3    C .default  0.15

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: design creel_design object interviews attached via add_interviews. Must include trip_status field distinguish complete incomplete trips. catch Bare column name catch data (supports tidy evaluation) effort Bare column name effort data (supports tidy evaluation) Optional tidy selector grouping variables. provided, performs TOST group independently. Overall validation passes overall test group tests pass equivalence. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method. Options: "taylor" (default), "bootstrap", "jackknife". Passed estimate_catch_rate. conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals (default: 0.95). Passed estimate_catch_rate. truncate_at Numeric minimum trip duration (hours) incomplete trip estimation. Default 0.5 hours (30 minutes). Passed estimate_catch_rate MOR estimator.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: creel_tost_validation S3 object (list) components: overall_test: List TOST results overall comparison (p_lower, p_upper, equivalence_passed, diff_estimate, equivalence_bounds) group_tests: Data frame per-group TOST results (present grouped estimation) equivalence_threshold: Numeric threshold used (e.g., 0.20 ±20\ passed: Logical indicating overall groups (applicable) passed equivalence recommendation: Character string usage recommendation metadata: List complete incomplete trip estimates, standard errors, confidence intervals, sample sizes

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: function estimates CPUE separately complete trips (using ratio--means) incomplete trips (using mean--ratios) via estimate_catch_rate. performs TOST test equivalence within specified threshold. Equivalence bounds calculated ±threshold * complete_trip_estimate. difference variance estimated using delta method: Var(complete - incomplete) = Var(complete) + Var(incomplete), assuming independence two samples. Sample size requirements: least 10 complete trips 10 incomplete trips required stable variance estimation TOST. function errors either sample size insufficient.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: package option tidycreel.equivalence_threshold controls equivalence threshold (default: 0.20 = ±20\ bounds equivalence ±threshold * complete_trip_estimate. example, default 20\ equivalence bounds 1.6 2.4 fish/hour. Users can set custom threshold: options(tidycreel.equivalence_threshold = 0.15)

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: TOST (Two One-Sided Tests) statistically appropriate method proving similarity two estimates. Unlike traditional hypothesis testing tests difference, TOST tests null hypothesis estimates differ threshold. Equivalence concluded one-sided tests reject null (p-values < 0.05). two tests : H0: complete - incomplete <= -threshold vs H1: complete - incomplete > -threshold H0: complete - incomplete >= threshold vs H1: complete - incomplete < threshold tests must reject (p < 0.05) equivalence. ensures estimates "close enough" considered equivalent practical purposes.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
code: # Create design with both complete and incomplete trips calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = rep(c("weekday", "weekend"), each = 2) ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  set.seed(123) interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(rep(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04"), each = 25)),   catch_total = rpois(100, lambda = 6),   hours_fished = runif(100, min = 2, max = 4),   trip_status = rep(c("complete", "incomplete"), each = 50),   trip_duration = runif(100, min = 2, max = 4) )  design_with_interviews <- add_interviews(design, interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 1 interview has zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> ℹ Added 100 interviews: 50 complete (50%), 50 incomplete (50%)  # Validate incomplete trips result <- validate_incomplete_trips(design_with_interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished ) print(result) #>  #> ── TOST Equivalence Validation Results ───────────────────────────────────────── #> Threshold: ±20% of complete trip estimate #> ✔ Validation PASSED #>  #> Recommendation: Validation passed: Safe to use incomplete trips for CPUE #> estimation in this dataset #>  #>  #> ── Overall Test ── #>  #> Complete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 2.046 #> Incomplete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 1.969 #> Difference: 0.077 #> Equivalence bounds: [-0.409, 0.409] #> TOST p-values: p_lower = 0.0049, p_upper = 0.0359 #> ✔ Overall equivalence: PASSED #>    # Grouped validation result_grouped <- validate_incomplete_trips(design_with_interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   by = day_type ) #> Warning: ! Only 0.0% of interviews are complete trips (threshold: 10%) #> ℹ Pollock et al. recommends >=10% complete trips for valid estimation #> ℹ Consider use_trips='diagnostic' to validate incomplete trip estimates print(result_grouped) #>  #> ── TOST Equivalence Validation Results ───────────────────────────────────────── #> Threshold: ±20% of complete trip estimate #> ✔ Validation PASSED #>  #> Recommendation: Validation passed: Safe to use incomplete trips for CPUE #> estimation in this dataset (overall and all groups passed equivalence) #>  #>  #> ── Overall Test ── #>  #> Complete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 2.046 #> Incomplete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 1.969 #> Difference: 0.077 #> Equivalence bounds: [-0.409, 0.409] #> TOST p-values: p_lower = 0.0049, p_upper = 0.0359 #> ✔ Overall equivalence: PASSED #>  #>  #> ── Per-Group Tests ── #>  #> 1/1 group passed equivalence #>   # Custom equivalence threshold options(tidycreel.equivalence_threshold = 0.15) # 15% threshold result_custom <- validate_incomplete_trips(design_with_interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished )

title: R Package GitHub Review Prompt
text: Review git diff R package, prioritizing GitHub delivery. Focus : Messages via cli, errors via rlang::abort(). Return types stable (tibble vs data.frame), attributes preserved. unexpected side effects (options, RNG, locale, working dir, connections). Use withr:: guards .exit(). Function signatures defaults consistent (including ...). S3/S4/R6/Rcpp compatibility; stable classes. Deprecations noted lifecycle (CRAN policy needed). Handles length-0/1, recycling, NA/NaN/Inf/NULL, factors vs character, UTF-8, Date/POSIXct + TZ. Tidy-eval safety ({{ }}, quosures). File paths portable (normalizePath(), tempfile()). Avoid accidental copies (copy--modify). Use preallocation. data.table -reference (:=) vs unintended copies. Parallel code portable (PSOCK Windows). Numeric tolerances appropriate. Invariants (classes/attributes) preserved. .onLoad/.onAttach mutate user state. Options/env vars namespaced. Add/adjust tests 0/1/NA/factors/TZ/locale/encoding. Snapshot outputs (CLI, printing). Error class assertions. Optional vdiffr plots. roxygen2 docs complete (@param/@return/@examples/@export). NAMESPACE regenerated. README install section (pak, remotes) current. NEWS.md tag changes. pkgdown builds. Minimal DESCRIPTION. CI green across OSes. Lints (lintr, styler) pass.

title: tidycreel 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Survey-first refactor: Estimation relies day-PSU designs as_day_svydesign() interview-level svydesign; legacy constructors (design_access, design_roving, design_repweights) removed. New effort estimators (survey-first): est_effort.instantaneous, est_effort.progressive, est_effort.aerial, est_effort.busroute_design design-based variance (supports svydesign svrepdesign). Aerial enhancements: visibility/calibration adjustments; optional post-stratification calibration via survey. estimate_effort(), estimate_cpue(), estimate_harvest() now error guidance new APIs. Replicate-weight helpers removed; use survey::.svrepdesign() day-PSU designs needed. Added “Survey Package Creel: Translator”. Added “Replicate Designs Creel Inference”. Updated “Getting Started”, “Effort (Survey-First)”, aerial examples use as_day_svydesign() new estimators. Docs/DevEx: Updated README, pkgdown navbar, AGENTS.md conventions; modernized CI workflows (R CMD check, lintr, pkgdown).
